table_notes: Footnote 1: Fees generally uniform within designated parameters but BKC reserves right to vary fees by negotiation. All payable via direct monthly withdrawal or electronic payment.
Footnote 2: Royalty — Base Fee means the greater of 4.5% of Gross Sales or the then-current standard undiscounted royalty. Multiple programs reduce this rate (see separate reduction tables).
Footnote 3: Advertising — as of 1/1/2025 set at 4.5%. Base Fee for advertising not to exceed the greater of 4.5% or then-current standard. Multiple programs reduce this rate.

Footnote 4: Rent — BKL (land + building): min annual rent typically 10% of capitalized site costs or 125% of BKC rent + 10% of costs. Percentage rent: 8.5% up to $133,333.33/mo, 10% above. 12% increases every 5 years (BKC-owned).
Footnote 5: Entity conversion — $5,000/Entity application; $1,000/restaurant for first 10; free for 11-20; $500 each 21+.
Footnote 6: Sales Impact Contribution — development dispute resolution procedures adopted with NFA. Arbitrator may award compensation to impacted existing franchisee.

Footnote 7: Digital Services Fee — covers ordering, delivery, and loyalty via BK app/website and designated third-party platforms. May increase/decrease once per year. Not deductible from Gross Sales.

title: 2024 Multi-Unit DIP — Royalty Reduction Schedule
table_notes: Applies to each Restaurant developed in BKoT Image under MTRA for 2024 Multi-Unit New Development Incentive Program.
Failure to open as scheduled resets royalty to then-current standard rate. Second failure terminates MTRA and increases all existing Restaurant royalties to then-current rate.

title: Development Agreement — Royalty Reduction (Standard Restaurants)
table_notes: Values represent reductions from the then-current Base Fee amount of royalty.
Base Fee means the greater of 4.5% of Gross Sales or the then-current standard undiscounted royalty.
Applies to standard (non-Excess) Restaurants under a Development Agreement.

title: Development Agreement — Royalty Reduction (Excess Restaurants)
table_notes: Values represent reductions from the then-current Base Fee amount of royalty.
Excess Restaurant is as defined in the Development Agreement.
More favorable reductions than standard restaurants.

title: 2024 Multi-Unit DIP — Advertising Contribution Reduction Schedule
table_notes: Applies to each Restaurant opened under MTRA for 2024 Multi-Unit New Development Program.
Base Fee for advertising means the amount determined by BKC, not to exceed the greater of 4.5% of Gross Sales or then-current standard.

title: Development Agreement — Advertising Reduction (Standard Restaurants)
table_notes: Values represent reductions from then-current Base Fee amount of advertising contribution.

title: Development Agreement — Advertising Reduction (Excess Restaurants)
table_notes: Values represent reductions from then-current Base Fee amount of advertising contribution for Excess Restaurants.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — Traditional Freestanding
rows: New Franchisees Training Fee, Travel & Living Expenses (2)
Cash, Inventory Control and Order Taking System (13)
Business Licenses, Utility Deposits, Lease Deposits and Payments (16)
table_notes: All payments are non-refundable.
Footnote 1: Fee may vary with term length. $50,000 for 20 years, $25,000 for 10 years. TRA deposit $5,000 or MTRA deposit $5,000-$10,000 credited against fee.
Footnote 2: Low estimate assumes Miami-based, no travel. High includes 12 trips and 60 days moderate room and board.
Footnote 3: If buying unimproved property for freestanding, $250,000-$2,000,000 typical.
Footnote 8: Low range is BK equipment only. Includes sound and security systems. Local smoke/odor ordinances may increase costs.
Footnote 10: Double Drive Thru required for freestanding. DDT may increase remodel costs $80,000-$120,000.
Footnote 11: Pre-opening wages assume managers 3 months, shift coordinators 10 weeks at 40 hrs/wk, other employees 2 weeks at 15 hrs/wk.
Footnote 15: Working capital covers first 3 months of operations.
Excludes real estate acquisition costs.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — Co-Brand (Non-Traditional)
table_notes: Co-brand costs relate only to the BK restaurant portion. Co-branded business costs not shown.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — In-Line / End Cap (Non-Traditional)
table_notes: In-line rent typically $1-$200/sq ft plus CAM $2-$80/sq ft.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — Mall Food Court (Non-Traditional)
table_notes: Decor Package shows '---' in both columns (not applicable). Mall food court rent $25-$688.55/sq ft plus CAM $6-$150/sq ft.
No landscaping line item for this format.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — Indoor MRS (Non-Traditional)
table_notes: MRS = Modular Retail System. Limited production capability, limited menu. No landscaping line item.

title: Estimated Initial Investment — Big-Box (Non-Traditional)
table_notes: Real property is projected 3 months rent at $35-$200/sq ft including CAM, occupying 300-2,500 sq ft.
No landscaping or zoning line items for Big-Box format.
Decor and Signage combined into single line item.
No successor franchise option for Big-Box.

title: Sales Distribution — Traditional and Non-Traditional Restaurants (Percentage at Sales Level)
table_notes: Period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2024.

Traditional: 5,837 reporting restaurants (4,774 franchisee, 1,063 company-owned). Excludes 76 company-owned and 161 franchisee temporarily closed, 1 company-owned and 109 franchisee permanently closed, 2 company-owned and 43 franchisee opened in 2024.

Non-Traditional: 741 franchisee-owned and 34 company-owned reporting (775 total). Company-owned non-traditional not separately broken out in table. Excludes 44 temporarily closed, 26 permanently closed, 29 opened in 2024.
Percentages represent percentage of restaurants at each sales level (not percentage of total sales).

title: Sales Distribution — Traditional and Non-Traditional Summary Statistics
table_notes: Period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2024.
Total traditional restaurants reporting: 5,837. Total non-traditional: 775.
THE SALES FIGURES DO NOT REFLECT COSTS OF SALES, OPERATING EXPENSES, OR OTHER COSTS.
Some Restaurants have sold these amounts. Your individual results may differ. There is no assurance you'll sell as much.

title: Sales Distribution — Fuel Co-Branded: Full Size and Large
table_notes: Period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2024. All franchisee-owned.
Full Size: 2300+ sq ft, seats 50-70. 60 total in system; 59 reporting (1 permanently closed excluded).
Large: 1500-2300 sq ft, seats 40-65. 138 total; 135 reporting (1 temporary closure, 1 permanent closure, 1 opened in 2024 excluded).
Percentage of Total Sales = total sales at range / total annual sales of all restaurants in category.

title: Sales Distribution — Fuel Co-Branded: Small and Kiosk
table_notes: Period: January 1, 2024 – December 31, 2024. All franchisee-owned.
Small: 1200-1500 sq ft, seats 30-40. 36 total; 34 reporting (1 temporary, 1 permanent closure excluded).
Kiosk: 200-1200 sq ft, seats 0-30. 86 total; 83 reporting (1 temporary, 2 permanent closures excluded).

title: Remodel Uplift — By Remodel Scope
table_notes: Sample: 893 Traditional Restaurants, CAPEX $650,000+, remodeled to Modern Image between 1/1/2018 and 12/31/2023.
Uplift measured vs. Control Restaurants (non-remodeled in same DMA with similar seasonality/trends).
Full = existing structure maintained, interior/exterior upgraded. Scrape & Rebuild = demolished and rebuilt in same location.
Restaurants operating 12-70 years depending on location.

title: Remodel Uplift — By Drive Thru Conversion Type
table_notes: 316 restaurants converted from single drive-thru to double drive-thru at time of remodel.
577 restaurants did not undergo drive-thru conversion.
Conversion to Double DT adds approximately 5.5 percentage points to average sales uplift.

title: Remodel Uplift — By Pre-Remodel Image Type
table_notes: Legacy = 1999, ROC, or similar image types (operating 13-63 years).
20/20 Light = 20/20 Light, 20/20 Standard, 20/20 Hybrid, or similar (operating 12-70 years).
Other = image type before remodel could not be established (operating 18-55 years).
Legacy-to-Modern shows significantly higher uplift than 20/20-to-Modern.

title: Remodel Uplift — By Pre-Remodel Annual Sales Level Range
table_notes: Restaurants classified by sales in 12-month period before remodel start date.
Uplift measured for 12-month period after remodel completion vs. Control Restaurants.
Restaurants operating 7-70 years depending on location.
Highest average uplift in $0.9M-$1.1M band (20.3%); lowest in >$1.9M band (9.5%).

title: Remodel Uplift — By Enrollment in Reclaim the Flame Program
table_notes: 118 restaurants enrolled in RTF that completed remodel on or before 12/31/2023 and were open for entire 2024.
RTF-enrolled Full remodels show higher average uplift (14.7%) vs. non-enrolled Full (11.0%).
RTF Scrape & Rebuild and non-enrolled Scrape show similar uplift levels (~26%).

title: Multi-Year Sales Uplift — Grouped Remodeled Restaurants
rows: 3 Years or More Post Remodel Performance
3 Years or More Post Remodel Performance
3 Years or More Post Remodel Performance
3 Years or More Post Remodel Performance
3 Years or More Post Remodel Performance
table_notes: Grouped by total number of years of post-remodel performance available as of 12/31/2024.
1 Year group: only 12 months of post-remodel data. 2 Years: only 24 months. 3+: at least 36 months.
Cumulative uplift measured vs. Control Restaurants for each year post-remodel.
Uplift persists and slightly increases in years 2-3 (total average: 12.3% → 12.6% → 13.4%).

title: Table No. 1 — Systemwide Outlet Summary
table_notes: On May 16, 2024, RBI acquired Carrols Restaurant Group, Inc., resulting in 1,023 franchised Restaurants becoming company-owned.
2024 franchised net change of -1,116 is overwhelmingly from the Carrols acquisition (1,023 reacquired), not organic closures.

title: Table No. 2 — Transfers of Outlets from Franchisees to New Owners (Summary)
table_notes: Figures do not include transfers where beneficial ownership does not change.
Full state-by-state detail spans pages 112-116 (50 states) but only totals extracted here.
2024 most active states: GA (57), MO (53), AL (44), IA (24), IL (20), TX (18).
reason_if_partial: State-by-state detail available on pages 112-116 but only national totals and top states extracted. Full 50-state rows would exceed practical table size.

title: Table No. 3 — Status of Franchise Outlets (Summary Totals)
table_notes: On May 16, 2024, RBI acquired Carrols Restaurant Group (1,023 restaurants) — accounts for nearly all 2024 reacquisitions.
Zero terminations in all three years.
2023 saw 270 ceased operations (other reasons) and 55 non-renewals — elevated attrition year even before Carrols.
Restaurants under limited license after FA termination/expiration may not be reflected as terminated.
'Non-renewed' may include mutual decisions or unilateral BKC refusal to offer successor franchise.
'Ceased other' includes offsets (new nearby restaurant), condemnation, mutual closures, unilateral franchisee action.
Some totals may not reconcile due to date posting overlaps, transfers, temporary closures.
reason_if_partial: Full state-by-state detail spans pages 116-121 (50 states, 3 years). Only national totals extracted. State detail available in source.

title: Table No. 4 — Status of Company-Owned Outlets (Summary Totals)
table_notes: On May 16, 2024, RBI acquired Carrols (1,023 restaurants). Prior company-owned were primarily FL (51 in 2022).
2024 company-owned presence expanded to 28 states from essentially just FL.
11 restaurants resold to franchisees in 2024 (refranchising pipeline beginning).
12 company-owned closures in 2024.
reason_if_partial: Full state-by-state detail spans pages 122-124 (28 states). Only national totals extracted.

rows: Franchise Agreements Signed But Outlets Not Opened

48 projected new franchised openings across approximately 25 states. Top states: TX (6), NY (5), GA (4), NJ (4), PA (4).

title: Fuel Co-Branded Restaurant Category Definitions
table_notes: Fuel Co-Branded = BK Restaurant attached to branded gas station (not truck stops or interstate travel plazas).
Many have a convenience store co-located.
320 total Fuel Co-Branded restaurants reporting, all franchisee-owned.
